scenes.narration: Bhai, Google ke SDE-2 round mein Linux Process Scheduling (CFS) pe ye trick poochhi jati hai. 30 second sun le.
Without understanding Linux Process Scheduling (CFS), systems fail at scale. Here's why it matters.
Think of Linux Process Scheduling (CFS) like a traffic cop at a busy intersection — directing flow efficiently.
The core mechanism of Linux Process Scheduling (CFS): it distributes work, manages state, and handles failures gracefully.
Key properties of Linux Process Scheduling (CFS): fault tolerance, horizontal scalability, and low latency.
Real-world example: companies like Netflix and Uber use Linux Process Scheduling (CFS) to serve millions of users daily.
Quick recap: Linux Process Scheduling (CFS) solves scale. Remember: distribute, monitor, and failover. That's the pattern.
